text.title: One-Way-Door Flagging Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of the decision_class fields in ADRs, the devil's-advocate review, and the quarterly miscall report.

field.name: adr.reversal_estimate_engineer_weeks
field.notes: 0 for trivial two-way; integer or float >= 1 for one-way

field.name: adr.reversal_estimate_breakdown
field.notes: keys: code, data_migration, vendor_exit, customer_impact, dependent_systems

forbidden.pattern: decision_class missing from any new ADR (pre-commit lint blocks)
decision_class = one-way-door AND merged_at less than 48 hours after cooldown_start
reversal_estimate_engineer_weeks omitted on one-way ADRs OR set to a token like TBD or unknown
classification_rationale shorter than 50 words (boilerplate label is not a classification)
devils_advocate_review absent at merge time on a one-way ADR

allowed-transformations.transformation: Shorten cooldown to 24h if the decision is time-critical (e.g. production-incident-driven) — requires engineering leader written approval logged in the ADR.
Aggregate the reversal_estimate_breakdown into a single number when posting the ADR summary; keep the breakdown in the file for the miscall review.

list.item: decision_class field set and matches the rationale paragraph
One-way-door ADRs have a 48h cooldown logged
Devil's-advocate reviewer named and rotated within quarter limits
Reversal estimate is a real number with breakdown, not a placeholder
Quarterly miscall report exists for each closed quarter
Quick-classification heuristics applied as a sanity check
